purpose: Validate native MORI inverse for compact combine weighted-token output before wiring it into TorchTitan autograd.

path_decision.mainline: Continue MORI-native balanced-MoE around the proven hot/cold/helper owner-compact layout.
path_decision.side_branch: Primus-Turbo TurboEPBackend remains bounded to raw-top-k/no-helper EP viability because it does not natively consume the winning hot/cold/helper owner-compact partition.

source_changes: tools/mori_native_weighted_combine_smoke.py: extended smoke to verify grad rows and grad top-score outputs

math.backward_grad_top_score: d_top_scores_flat[flat] += sum_d dY[token(flat), d] * source_row[row, d]

build.command: docker run --rm --network host --ipc host --device=/dev/kfd --device=/dev/dri --group-add video --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --entrypoint bash -v /scratch:/scratch -w /scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527/sources/references/mori/build onenexus/nexus-titan:rocm722-pytorch-nightly-mori-devdeps -lc 'ninja -j8'
notes: The devdeps image entrypoint is titan-run, so --entrypoint bash is required.
The CMake cache uses absolute /scratch paths, so /scratch must be mounted into the container.
Rebuilt libmori_ops.so and libmori_pybinds.so, then copied them into python/mori/.

read: The native MORI backward primitive is correct in the isolated compact owner-row contract, including cross-rank flat-position ordering. It is not yet a throughput result; the next gate is wiring TorchTitan autograd to use it instead of _weighted_scatter_flat_backward, then profiling whether it removes source-rank row/flat-position bridge pressure without increasing lifetime or memory.
